question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted transaction whose consensusTimestamp is manipulated to be far in the past, causing prune() to delete legitimate ContractResult records that should still be retained, resulting in permanent data loss of valid transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user flood the network with transactions that produce ContractResult entries with artificially low consensusTimestamp values, causing prune() to silently delete them on the next retention cycle, effectively erasing evidence of those transactions from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction that causes the consensusTimestamp boundary used by prune() to be set to a future value, triggering mass deletion of all ContractResult rows up to that timestamp and causing a denial-of-service against historical contract result queries?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction that causes a duplicate consensusTimestamp to be inserted into the contract_result table, and since isNew() always returns true (bypassing Hibernate's existence check), cause a primary key collision that corrupts or overwrites an existing ContractResult record?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a tampered consensusTimestamp that exactly matches an existing record's primary key, and because isNew() unconditionally returns true, cause the repository to attempt a duplicate insert that either silently overwrites or throws an unhandled exception, disrupting the importer pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: processContractResult()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a ContractCall transaction with a spoofed senderId field in the ContractFunctionResult protobuf, causing the persisted ContractResult.senderId to point to an arbitrary account, leading to incorrect attribution of contract execution and potential bypass of payer-based access controls in downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: processContractResult()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a manipulated transactionHash field, causing the stored ContractResult.transactionHash to differ from the actual transaction hash, breaking hash-based lookups and allowing the attacker to make a fraudulent transaction appear to have a different identity in the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: processContractResult()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a crafted transactionNonce value that collides with an existing nonce for the same sender, causing the mirror node to store a ContractResult with a duplicate nonce, corrupting Ethereum-compatible nonce ordering and enabling replay-attack confusion in EVM-compatible clients?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: processContractResult()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a manipulated transactionResult code, causing the stored ContractResult.transactionResult to reflect a false success or failure status, misleading downstream applications that rely on this field to determine whether a contract execution succeeded?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: processContractResult()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction whose ContractFunctionResult.errorMessage contains SQL injection or special characters, and since errorMessage is stored without sanitization, cause corruption or injection into downstream systems that process the error_message column from the contract_result table?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: processContractResult()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with an oversized ContractFunctionResult.bloom field, causing the setBloom() method to store an unexpectedly large byte array in the database, leading to storage exhaustion or denial-of-service against the mirror node importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: setBloom(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction whose bloom filter bytes exactly match EMPTY_BLOOM (256 zero bytes), causing setBloom() to store an empty byte array instead of the real bloom, silently discarding log filter data and making log-based event queries return incorrect results for that transaction?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: processContractResult()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a tampered contractId in the ContractFunctionResult, causing the stored ContractResult.contractId to reference a different or non-existent contract, breaking contract-based lookups and potentially associating malicious execution results with a legitimate contract's history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: processContractResult()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a manipulated gasLimit value, causing the stored ContractResult.gasLimit to be arbitrarily high or low, misleading gas analytics tools and potentially bypassing gas-based throttling checks in downstream consumers that rely on the mirror node's stored gasLimit?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: processContractResult()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction with a manipulated gasUsed value in the ContractFunctionResult, causing the stored ContractResult.gasUsed to be incorrect, corrupting gas consumption analytics and potentially causing updateGasConsumed() to compute a wrong gasConsumed value that is then persisted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: calculateGasConsumed()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a crafted functionParameters payload containing all non-zero bytes, causing calculateGasConsumed() to compute an inflated intrinsicGas value (via ISTANBUL_TX_DATA_NON_ZERO_COST * nonZeros), resulting in a stored gasConsumed that is significantly higher than actual, corrupting gas accounting records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: calculateGasConsumed()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a crafted initcode payload of maximum size, causing calculateGasConsumed() to overflow the long arithmetic in TX_BASE_COST + TX_DATA_ZERO_COST * zeros + ISTANBUL_TX_DATA_NON_ZERO_COST * nonZeros + TX_CREATE_EXTRA, resulting in a negative or wrapped gasConsumed value being stored in the ContractResult?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: addDefaultEthereumTransactionContractResult()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a failed Ethereum transaction with a spoofed transactionHash, causing addDefaultEthereumTransactionContractResult() to store the attacker-controlled hash as the ContractResult.transactionHash, enabling hash collision attacks against Ethereum-compatible transaction lookup APIs?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: processContractResult()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a crafted ContractFunctionResult.callResult containing arbitrary bytes, causing the stored ContractResult.callResult to contain attacker-controlled data that is later served to API consumers as the authoritative contract execution output?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: processContractResult()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a crafted ContractFunctionResult.functionResult (full protobuf bytes), causing the stored ContractResult.functionResult to contain attacker-controlled protobuf data that is later deserialized by downstream consumers, potentially triggering protobuf parsing vulnerabilities?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: processContractResult()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a manipulated transactionIndex, causing the stored ContractResult.transactionIndex to be out of order or collide with another transaction's index, corrupting the ordering of transactions within a block as seen by Ethereum-compatible clients querying the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toContractTransactionHash()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a null transactionHash, causing toContractTransactionHash() to build a ContractTransactionHash with a null hash field, corrupting the contract_transaction_hash table and breaking hash-based transaction lookups for that contract?
